parts.title: Transcript
parts.annotated_transcript_html:
Speaker 1: Danny
Hi, Sarah.

Speaker 2: Sarah
Oh, hi, Danny.

Speaker 1: Danny
I was thinking, when I finish my degree, I'd like to do a short work placement with a company to get some experience.

Speaker 2: Sarah
Oh, an internship, you mean?

Speaker 1: Danny
Yeah. If I'm applying for a job in the future after my placement, then the company would say, OK, this guy is organised and can clearly think ahead and decide what he wants to do. And that could give me an advantage over other candidates for the same job, even if they've got more experience than me in other areas.

Speaker 2: Sarah
Well, maybe. You interested in an internship?

Speaker 1: Danny
Definitely, but it'd have to be with a huge corporation.

Speaker 2: Sarah
Right. Why?

Speaker 1: Danny
Because they tend to be multinational, possibly with openings for interns in offices across the globe. So I could choose where to go, rather than staying in the UK.

Speaker 2: Sarah
Hmm, but wouldn't you just end up working with people you never see again?

Speaker 1: Danny
Maybe, but internships aren't only about networking, are they?

Speaker 2: Sarah
True. I was glad when our tutor told us about some of the agencies that can arrange internships for us.

Speaker 1: Danny
Yes, but there are so many that it might be hard to choose one, especially because most work in all sectors rather than specialising in a specific field.

Speaker 2: Sarah
Exactly, and most just say exactly the same on their websites.

Speaker 1: Danny
Right, hard to tell them apart.

Speaker 2: Sarah
So I reckon the way to go is to reach out to someone who arranged a placement through them.

Speaker 1: Danny
Right, and ask them what the agency was really like.

Speaker 2: Sarah
Exactly. The tutor said she could help put us in touch with some recent graduates for that.

Speaker 1: Danny
Sure. Having said that, my cousin, right, she qualified as an accountant and then got a placement in a construction company in the Middle East.

Speaker 2: Sarah
Doing accounting, presumably.

Speaker 1: Danny
Yeah. The thing is, though, she didn't bother registering with any agencies. She just went straight to the HR people in the firm and asked for a placement with them.

Speaker 2: Sarah
A speculative application, then.

Speaker 1: Danny
Yeah. Did it work?

Speaker 2: Sarah
Yeah, she got one. So I might just do what she did.

Speaker 1: Danny
Right. Another option is a remote internship.

Speaker 2: Sarah
What, an online internship where you never actually go into the office?

Speaker 1: Danny
That's right. So you could do it one day a week while doing your degree.

Speaker 2: Sarah
But if you never meet your colleagues or enter a work environment, I can't see the purpose of a remote internship.

Speaker 1: Danny
Actually, I think you're missing the point. Could be a great way to test it for a while, you know, working with the company. And once the initial internship is over, then, if you're happy with the company, you could go on to a face-to-face internship with them as the next step.

Speaker 2: Sarah
Well, I suppose that's true.
